architecture_decisions.recommendation: Create a modular Python translation script with pluggable translation service adapters (Google Translate, DeepL) and configurable language targets. Preserve code blocks, image references, and links during translation.
architecture_decisions.rationale: The repository contains technical content with code snippets that must not be translated. A modular design allows maintainers to switch translation services based on cost/accuracy and supports incremental language expansion without rewriting core logic.
alternatives_considered: Markdown parsing with regex instead of AST

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Follow existing translation patterns: create language-suffixed files (README-es.md) in root and mirror this pattern in solution directories. Do not create separate language directories.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Consistency with existing Chinese (zh-Hans) and Japanese (ja) translations reduces cognitive load for contributors. Flat structure with suffix pattern is already used and works with existing tooling like generate-epub.sh.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Implement structure-preserving validation tests that compare original and translated files for identical code blocks, image paths, and link anchors. Use pytest for test framework.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Technical accuracy is critical - code snippets must remain unchanged across translations. Automated validation prevents introduction of broken links or corrupted code during translation process.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Implement GitHub Actions workflow that runs translation validation tests on PRs, checks for broken image references, and validates markdown structure.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Automated quality gates ensure translated content maintains repository standards without manual review overhead. GitHub Actions is native to the platform and aligns with existing contribution workflows.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Extend generate-epub.sh to accept language parameter and update epub-metadata.yaml with multilingual metadata. Process language-suffixed markdown files for EPUB generation.
architecture_decisions.rationale: The existing EPUB generation script is simple and effective. Parameterizing it for languages avoids duplication while supporting the feature requirement for multilingual EPUBs.
alternatives_considered: Separate EPUB generation scripts per language

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Use environment variables and a config file (translate_config.yaml) for API keys, target languages, and exclusion patterns. Never commit API keys.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Translation services require API keys that must remain secret. Environment-based configuration supports CI/CD integration and allows contributors to use their own keys.

tickets.title: Create translation automation script with Google Translate adapter
tickets.description: Develop translate_markdown.py script that can translate markdown files while preserving code blocks (```), image references, and links. Implement Google Translate API adapter with configurable exclusion patterns.

tickets.title: Add Spanish translation scaffolding and example content
tickets.description: Create README-es.md in root directory and corresponding README-es.md files in key solution directories (mint, pastebin, web_crawler) following the same structure as README-zh-Hans.md. Include placeholders for automated translation.

tickets.title: Update contribution guidelines with translation workflow
tickets.description: Add translation contribution section to CONTRIBUTING.md explaining how to use the translation script, maintain code snippet integrity, and follow the language suffix pattern.

tickets.title: Implement translation validation test suite
tickets.description: Create pytest tests that validate translated files maintain identical code blocks, image references (images/*.png), and internal links as their English counterparts.

tickets.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD for translation validation
tickets.description: Create GitHub Actions workflow that runs translation tests on PRs, validates markdown structure, and checks for broken image links in translated content.

tickets.title: Extend EPUB generation for multiple languages
tickets.description: Modify generate-epub.sh to accept language parameter (e.g., --lang es) and update epub-metadata.yaml to include multilingual metadata. Ensure script processes correct language-suffixed markdown files.

tickets.title: Add DeepL translation adapter and configuration
tickets.description: Implement alternative DeepL translation adapter to provide translation service options. Update translate_config.yaml to support multiple service configuration.

tickets.title: Implement batch translation and progress tracking
tickets.description: Add batch processing capability to translate_markdown.py for translating multiple files/directories. Include progress bars and skip-already-translated logic.

technology_choices.choice: Google Translate API (with DeepL as alternative)
technology_choices.rationale: Google Translate offers broad language support and reliable quality. Making it configurable with DeepL alternative provides flexibility for maintainers who may prefer DeepL's technical translation accuracy.
pytest is the standard Python testing framework with rich plugin ecosystem. It's lightweight and integrates well with CI/CD pipelines.
The repository is already on GitHub, and GitHub Actions provides native CI/CD with free tier for open source. Existing .github directory suggests this is the preferred platform.
YAML is human-readable and widely used in the Python ecosystem. The existing epub-metadata.yaml shows YAML is already used in the codebase.

technology_choices.choice: Python's markdown library with custom extensions
technology_choices.rationale: The standard markdown library can parse code blocks and structure reliably. Custom extensions can handle the specific preservation requirements for technical content.
